content:
You may not have noticed, but the US just passed the biggest climate change legislation in a long time, with provisions on saving the ozone layer and helping solar, wind and nuclear energy, battery storage and carbon capture.

  • John Baez, Consolidated Appropriations Act 2021.

Woah, totally not expecting this. Great news!!
How did this happen? It seems a major blow to Trump's administration line.
I imagine nothing incredible happened, it's just that I'm ignorant of how the US government works.
Trump himself is completely uninvolved in this massive legislation: he's sitting around talking with crackpots who want to stage a coup.
And his administration also seems strangely uninvolved.

So it's mainly been a battle between Republicans and Democrats in Congress, and it seems they found a compromise that makes them happy enough to sign a huge bill... at the very last minute, right before the government would have to shut down and millions of people get evicted. (About 12 million people are behind on their rent payments.)
The bill is huge and contains lots of stuff to make many people in Congress happy.

I see. I'm familiar with this type of legislation, the Italian government just approved one like it: everybody agrees to stuff it with the things they want and to not obstruct the others from doing so. You get a big fat file and that's it
Yes, we've been doing this since the 50s and as you can see the consequence is that the country is crumbling. xD
Well, it seems that the bill has not been signed into law yet.

Yup, Trump is now trying to stir up trouble. I said the bill passed, which is one step.

The good news is that Trump is not particularly complaining about the climate-related stuff. Please: nobody tell him it's in there.
:face_palm:🏻‍♂️ I should've kept my mouth shout
That's a different bill, of course. And it was passed by a margin that could override a veto.
It funds the Defense Department; Trump is saying he'll only pass it if it includes legislation that regulates social media companies like Facebook. :shrug:
Ouch, I didn't even notice it was another one :joy: BBC News sent me the notification and I've just read an article about the consolidated appropriation act
In case someone in the world hasn't heard yet: Trump finally signed the big bill with all the good climate change legislation.
